programme.desc: Louie recruits Storkules for his professional monster extermination business, while Storkules faces his greatest challenge: being Donald’s roommate.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Boyz Crazy
programme.desc: Mabel joins a secret knitting club and is pressured by her new friends to gossip, but when she tells them that Dipper likes Wendy, she inadvertently puts Dipper's life in danger.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Love God
programme.desc: Mabel takes matchmaking too far when she steals a love potion from a real Love God.

programme.title: DuckTales: The House of the Lucky Gander
programme.desc: Donald competes with his cousin Gladstone Gander to impress Louie at a mysterious resort in Macaw, while Scrooge tries to lure the kids away from the resort's endless distractions.

programme.title: Mickey Mouse Shorts: Roughin' It/ Turkish Delights/ No Reservations
Mickey Mouse Shorts: Doggone Biscuits/ The Boiler Room/ Dancevidaniya
Big City Greens: Trailer Trouble/ Mansion Madness
programme.desc: Nancy's trailer is stolen by her old biker gang, and the kids resolve to get it back. Cricket house-sits for Remy, but things go awry when the rest of the Greens visit.

programme.title: Amphibia: A Night at the Inn/ Wally and Anne
programme.desc: The Plantars stay at an inn for a night, but Polly discovers the caretakers aren’t what they seem. Everyone calls Anne crazy when she claims to have seen the Moss Man.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania: The Series: Mummyfest Destiny/ Bandages of Brothers
programme.desc: Pedro's amulet has the ability to stop time, ensuring he's always first up at the buffet. When Mavis attempts to use it, however, trouble ensues. Since Pedro and Hank aren't getting along, Mavis and Wendy suggest they look for new roommates.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania: The Series: Brain Drain/ Hair Today, Gone Tomorrow
programme.desc: Aunt Lydia snags Pandora's Box from Mavis and asks Zombie Isaac Newton to figure out how to open it. Unfortunately, a brain swap between him and Hank creates bigger problems. When Medusa comes to Hotel Transylvania, Aunt Lydia's hair acts up.

programme.title: Big Hero 6: The Series: Fan Friction
programme.desc: When Karmi starts posting fan fiction about Big Hero 6, depicting Hiro as her boyfriend, Hiro is embarrassed and wants her to stop.

programme.title: Big Hero 6: The Series: Write Turn Here
programme.desc: Hiro is forced to write a story for his class and decides to write a Big Hero 6 story. Meanwhile, strange versions of Noodle Burger Boy attack the team.

programme.title: Polaris: Player Select: Marki-Fired!
programme.desc: A compilation of top gaming videos from YouTube stars.

programme.title: Parker Plays
programme.desc: YouTube star Parker Coppins shares his hilarious gameplay commentary.

programme.title: DuckTales: The Spear of Selene!
programme.desc: Donald is dragged into a feud between Scrooge and Zeus, while Dewey and Webby search for an artifact that may provide the truth about Dewey's mother.

programme.title: DuckTales: Terror of the Terra-firmians
programme.desc: Huey and Webby explore an abandoned subway tunnel to prove the existence of an underground species, while Mrs. Beakley grows suspicious of Lena’s potentially corrupting influence.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Scary-oke
programme.desc: Kyle wants to be smart like his younger sister. When The Cortex overhears this, he decides to exploit Kyle's dream. Then, Motho Jr. doesn't want to wreak havoc like the stereotypical movie monster his dad is, he just wants to dance.

programme.title: Amphibia: Toad Tax/ Prison Break
programme.desc: Anne befriends toad soldiers to gain respect in Wartwood, but must choose between her new respect and her frog family. Far from Wartwood, Anne’s friend Sasha is captured by toads.

programme.title: DuckTales: The Golden Spear!
programme.desc: As Della rebuilds her ship, Penumbra becomes convinced Della’s planning an attack. Meanwhile, Donald’s nap keeps getting interrupted by adventure.

programme.title: Marvel's Spider-Man: Brain Drain
programme.desc: Spider-Man and Miles Morales discover Doc Ock, whose mind has been hiding inside the metal body of The Living Brain, has been behind the mysterious bounty for Spider-Man's head.

programme.title: Big Hero 6: The Series: Baymax Returns: Part 1
programme.desc: Hiro thinks Baymax is lost forever, until he finds the chip that Tadashi designed to create Baymax. Hiro immediately gets to work rebuilding his friend.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Rated Cricket / Homeshare Hoedown
programme.desc: On the trip to Big City movie multiplex, Cricket thinks he’s too mature for a kids’ movie, so he sneaks into a grown-up movie. Cricket registers his house on a homeshare website.

programme.title: Beyblade Burst Turbo: Achilles vs Roktavor!
programme.desc: It’s the quarterfinals of the Lúinor Cup and Aiger is up against Cap’n! Before they battle, the two of them follow Hae-jin Oh to a secret training spot as Hae-jin teaches Aiger an important lesson.

programme.title: Pokémon: Sun & Moon - Ultra Adventures: Battle Royal 151
programme.desc: The Alola Pokémon League is off to a rousing start with a Battle Royal competition among friends and rivals both old and new.

programme.desc: Exposing the traumatic stories of migrant children detained under Trump's immigration policies. An investigation with The Associated Press into the widespread consequences – and business – of the mass confinement of migrant children.

programme.title: The Warrior Tradition
programme.desc: Learn the heartbreaking, inspiring and largely untold story of Native Americans in the United States military. This film relates the stories of Native American warriors from their own points of view -- stories of service, pain, courage and fear.

programme.title: Independent Lens: The Interpreters
programme.desc: More than 50,000 local interpreters helped protect U.S. troops on the ground. In the aftermath of war, some have been able to leave their home countries and reach safety, while others still languish in hiding and fear for their lives.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow: Orlando (Hour Two)
programme.desc: Learn more about vintage and antique items, including a 1965 NASA 'Corned Beef' archive, a Korean The Water Moon Viewing Guanyin painting, and a 1918 WWI peach can label letter.

programme.title: Sit and Be Fit: Yardsticks and Bands
programme.desc: Mary Ann shows a unique warm-up routine and then focuses on upper and lower body strengthening.

programme.title: Arthur: The Squirrels/ Fern and Persimmony Glitchet
programme.desc: Arthur and his friends watch a horror movie; Mr. Glitchet teaches Fern about creativity.

programme.title: Ready Jet Go!: Who Messed Up the Treehouse?/ Fact or Fiction?
programme.desc: Each kid brings their personal project up to the tree house, and each contributes to a huge mess. Sean and Sydney set out to show Mindy how to separate fact from fiction through research.

programme.title: Nature Cat: A Jump to Remember/ Star Gazers
programme.desc: Nature Cat falls asleep when he and his friends go star-gazing to see some shooting stars.

programme.title: Splash and Bubbles: Partner Pals/ Ultimate Hide and Seek
programme.desc: Splash and Bubbles have an argument and decide not to play together for a while.

programme.title: Molly of Denali: Sap Season/ Book of Mammoths
programme.desc: It’s been a long winter in Qyah, and everyone is out of birch syrup. Molly and her Dad are shocked when Travis, a tourist, announces that the goal of his expedition is to find a living woolly mammoth.

programme.title: Xavier Riddle and the Secret Museum: I Am Mary Shelley/ I Am Harry Houdini
programme.desc: Xavier goes back in time to meet spooky storyteller Mary Shelley before a Nature Troop campout.

programme.title: Let's Go Luna!: Glocken Around the Clock/ Good Knight
programme.desc: In Munich, Leo is put in charge of baby Mathilda for the day and finds it takes a special kind of Glockenspiel to make her happy; I Munich, when Honey pretends to be a hamster in distress, Carmen and the others must rescue her from Neuschwanstein Castle.

programme.title: Peg + Cat: The Potty Problem/ The Butter Problem
programme.desc: Peg and Cat learn certain math skills by doing farm chores to compete in a butter sculpting contest.

programme.title: Daniel Tiger's Neighborhood: Daniel Thinks of Others/ Daniel Thinks of What Margaret Needs
programme.desc: While making a picture for his family, Daniel ends up using all of the glitter. Daniel and Miss Elaina want to play their musical instruments but Margaret needs to sleep.

programme.title: Sesame Street: Elmo Goes to Chicken School
programme.desc: Elmo helps Professor Buck Awe teach a class of chickens using strategies he learned in preschool.

programme.title: Pinkalicious & Peterrific: Best Day Ever/ Catchy Song
programme.desc: Pop star Jonah Rose stops by Pinkville, and Pinkalicious and Peter are excited to see him do the 'Jonah Jump'; Pinkalicious writes the catchiest song that everyone sings, and tries to write an even catchier one to get it out of their heads.

programme.title: The Great British Baking Show: Advanced Dough
programme.desc: Mary and Paul up the ante to see which bakers will rise to the challenge. The competitors must make enriched sweet fruit loaves for their Signature, followed by a Technical challenge that stretches them to the limit.

programme.title: What the Durrells Did Next: A Masterpiece Special
programme.desc: Learn the definitive true story behind one of the best-loved families in TV drama, and see how Corfu shaped the personalities and ambitions of each Durrell. It’s an outlandish story of expeditions, fame, heartbreak and a zoo.

programme.title: Masterpiece Classic: Poldark
programme.desc: Demelza discovers the French are smuggling arms into Cornwall. Ross is determined to take vengeance on Merceron and Hanson for framing Ned. Caroline puts her insecurities aside to support Kitty’s battle against Merceron, drawing attention to herself.

programme.title: Secrets of Underground London
programme.desc: Underneath London lies a secret, hidden world, all but forgotten by the millions of people above. Two thousand years of history is uncovered, including ancient caves, perfectly preserved Roman remains, mysterious rivers and gruesome plague pits.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Thought Criminal
programme.desc: Under the guidance of Lieutenant Murphy, Sergeant Benson and the SVU take an active approach to catching sex offenders. They target Simon Wilkes, a well-regarded photographer with a secret life.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Spring Awakening
programme.desc: Amaro's recklessness once again makes him the target on Internal Affairs, and he has no choice but to face the consequences. A rape investigation leads a suspect to Sergeant Benson who may change her life forever.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Glasgowman's Wrath
programme.desc: Young friends sneak into the woods to search for a mythical creature. When only one of them returns, gravely injured, the SVU team work to locate a suspect and to distinguish the fantasy stories from the reality stories.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: The Good Girl
programme.desc: Benson works to uncover a pregnant teenager's secret when she refuses the name the baby's father.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit: Exchange
programme.desc: Benson helps a teenaged exchange student after she's assaulted by a cab driver.

programme.title: The Purge: House of Mirrors
programme.desc: In the world of The Purge, Ryan, Marcus, Esme, and Ben learn that some lines can't be un-crossed.

programme.title: Treadstone: The Bentley Lament
programme.desc: Bentley discovers the truth while Doug goes on a dangerous mission and Tara confronts her past.

programme.desc: Alex helps former four-time heavyweight champion, Evander Holyfield. The champ with a heart of gold has definitely had some missteps and his $250 million fortune is dwindling fast. It's up to Alex to rebuild his brand before it's too late.

programme.title: Treadstone: The Bentley Lament
programme.desc: Bentley discovers the truth while Doug goes on a dangerous mission and Tara confronts her past.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: I Remember Her Now
programme.desc: The body of a young girl is found crushed in an abandoned building after she runs away from a treatment centre for troubled girls. Voight sends Halstead to work undercover inside the centre, where he believes something shady is happening.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Seven Indictments

programme.desc: The team examines a corpse found floating in a pool; Warrick investigates a mob hit.

programme.desc: On a cold December night in 1993, Rose Larner stopped in a convenience store on her way to her boyfriend’s house. She was never seen or heard from again.

programme.desc: The deaths of a child and a truck driver in the same hospital on the same day are investigated to see if there is a link between them.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Knot for Everyone

programme.title: Something's Killing Me: Angel of Death
programme.desc: Hospital authorities and police investigate the mysterious deaths of several patients.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Hair of the Dog

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: Atlanta: Brides by the Numbers

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: Atlanta: Bride Overboard
programme.desc: Bride Andi and her fiancé struggle to agree on the perfect gown for their destination, sailboat wedding.

programme.desc: Youth Minster Tameria wants to wear a simple dress on her big day, but her fashion-forward family has other ideas. Hailey puts aside her sexy dress vision to please her future mother-in-law. Traditional bride Katie considers an unexpected gown alteration.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: Atlanta: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Jenee has every detail set for her big day, except for the dress. Country girl Jessica has a specific vision in mind for her gown, but her sister-in-law's city taste could stand in the way. At her fitting, Leslie is still looking for her mom's approval.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress: Atlanta: Worth the Weight
programme.desc: Optimistic bride Amy falls in love with a budget-busting gown and must convince Lori to let her try it on. Ambitious bride Ericia puts pressure on herself when she tries to lose 20 to 30 pounds and fit into a small dress before her big day.

programme.desc: The nine flaxen-haired children in the Plath family have never had a soda, don't know who Spiderman or Tom Brady is and have never watched TV, living remotely in rural Georgia with their 'follow their own rules' parents Kim and Barry Plath.

programme.title: Counting On: The Great Duggar Campout
programme.desc: Jinger and Jeremy go house hunting in L.A. but with pricier real estate and small square footage, they may not be able to find the perfect place. Meanwhile in Arkansas, the family is going camping. Later at dinner, one couple makes a special announcement.

programme.desc: In order to avoid more tension in the family, Kim allows Moriah to go to San Francisco with Olivia. From the moment they land Moriah quickly realizes she's a long way from south Georgia. Ethan finds creative ways to pass the time while Olivia is gone.

programme.title: OutDaughtered: Big Decisions in the Big Country
programme.desc: It's Adam's birthday and the family is celebrating with a weekend trip at a Dude Ranch. The Busbys must make a difficult decision about the girls' schooling.

programme.title: OutDaughtered: Raising Quints 101
programme.desc: The Busbys have become experts on parenting quintuplets, and are ready to teach the world.

programme.title: Extreme Cheapskates: Feast or Famine
programme.desc: Cooking instructor Ron Maestri shows us how to cook for lobster taste on a tunafish budget. Rocker Goldy Locks converted an abandoned hospital into her home, for just the fraction of the price.

programme.title: Extreme Cheapskates: Father Knows Debt
programme.desc: Patriarch Raul Pinto scours through vacuum canisters at the car wash in hopes of finding spare change. Kia Cambridge limits herself to one piece of gum, every three days.

programme.title: Extreme Cheapskates: Cheap Hair Day
programme.desc: Kelley saves money by insulating her entire house with plastic. Now, she's bringing her Pilates studio into the home to save $800 per month. Pelin cuts hair and costs at her salon and must find cheap bridal party products to trim her budget.

programme.title: Extreme Cheapskates: The Grass is Always Cheaper
programme.desc: Jamie's entire garden is made up solely of silk flowers. Now, she aims to replace her whole lawn with artificial grass, to eliminate her landscaping bill. Justin tries to land a job in sales, where he promises to save his employer tons of dough.

programme.title: Long Island Medium: I Don't Believe
programme.desc: In Theresa's line of work, she constantly has to deal with skeptics. In this episode, spouses and relatives drag their non-believer family members to Theresa, to get them to change their minds.

programme.title: Long Island Medium: Was It My Fault?
programme.desc: Over the years, Theresa has encountered dozens of people who feel they have played a part in the death of a loved one. Theresa connects these people with their loved ones to help them deal with their feelings of self-blame.

programme.title: Long Island Medium: Haunted Houses
programme.desc: In an episode the likes of which we've not seen in years, Theresa visits and cleanses the homes of people who believe they are haunted. First, Theresa visits a home believed to be visited by the previous owner.

programme.title: Long Island Medium: Healing the Anger
programme.desc: Theresa visits with people who are so consumed with anger over the death of loved ones that they're finding it difficult to move on with their lives. First, Theresa visits with a guilt-ridden man who wished his brother dead right before he overdosed.

programme.title: Untold Stories of the ER: Stabbed in the Heart
programme.desc: A stabbing victim's heart has a hole in it and must be pumped by hand to keep him alive.

programme.title: Untold Stories of the ER: Chocolate Pudding

programme.title: Gypsy Sisters: A Naughty or Nice Christmas

programme.title: Gypsy Sisters: The Name That Started a War
programme.desc: Now living under one roof, Kayla and Annie are enjoying the single life; a pregnant Mellie has moved in with Kayla and Annie leaving Nettie and JoAnn to question her allegiance, which reignites old wounds.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Tooth and Consequences
programme.desc: Joe, Sal, Murr and Q spread the love as attendants in a bridal dress shop much to the dismay of the brides-to-be, and break a sacred guy-code in the process. Plus, they attempt to sway frightened focus groups with fragrance campaigns that stink.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Swim Shady
programme.desc: Find out how long Q was 'sleeping' before the guys decided to have him stay asleep for 30 minutes.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Lady and the Tramp
programme.desc: The guys us a two-way mirror to get each other to crack, and tonight's loser gets taken for a ride that ends at the Statue of Liberty.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Brother of the Sisterhood
programme.desc: The guys play helpless oddballs in a waiting room, then ask grocery shoppers for cash. Then, tonight's loser becomes the number one enemy of women everywhere.

programme.title: The Misery Index: He's Crushing His Man Cave Game
programme.desc: The Tenderloins witness the most embarrassing way to ruin a wedding.

programme.title: The Misery Index: That Milk Must Have Turned by Now
programme.desc: Contestants guess the misery of some traumatic real-life events for the chance at $30,000.

programme.title: The Misery Index: How Did It Get in There?
programme.desc: Learn about the dumbest way to break up with someone and the gross dream that Sal needs to 'soldier forward' from. Then, a pantless performer and a vertical face planter go all out to be masters of misery.

programme.title: The Misery Index: Men of Intellect
programme.desc: Joe and Q become 'gentlemen', a little piggy goes to the emergency room, and a man rides the elevator from hell as a clumsy paramedic and accidental sexter battle to be masters of misery.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Dover and Out
programme.desc: The guys survey employees with odd questions, and tonight's loser unveils a mural in a small town whose residents react with big city rage.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: The Marathon Man
programme.desc: Find out which joker has pet parakeets, why the crew had to work in a truck down the block while filming at a bagel shop and who got to sound the start horn for Murr's race across the Brooklyn Bridge.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: No Good Deed
programme.desc: Find out what after-school activity Joe's mom forbid him from doing, why filming nearly halted during the jack asses challenge and what last minute idea made it into Sal and Joe's charity funds punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Turning the Tables
programme.desc: Find out how the Jokers keep their cover while filming at the mall, and Q's favorite VR word. Plus, learn how long Joe was sore after the table-busting punishment.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins a Sitcom
programme.desc: Adam tackles classic television stereotypes, from the racism behind public pools, to the 'model minority' myth of Asian Americans, to the on-screen toxic masculinity that's masking the problems young men face today.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins America
programme.desc: Adam lets his freedom flag fly by examining why America doesn’t have higher rates of social mobility. He then uncovers the flaws within the constitution and examines the country’s progression and regression.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Bull Shiatsu
programme.desc: Go behind the scenes to find out what word Joe uses as slang for cash, which prop did Q have to handle with care and how Joe managed to keep cool during his chair punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: To Hatch a Predator
programme.desc: A last-minute prop request that caused a scramble, where Murr’s fetching housecoat came from, and who was offered a job at the children’s museum following Q’s turtle punishment.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins a Night Out
programme.desc: Adam and Emily prepare for a big night out by revealing the marketing ploy that led to women shaving and the fashion agendas that created pockets. Then, the two friends pour back the truth on alcohol consumption.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Doing Good
programme.desc: Adam takes down the do-gooders and reveals how 'Teach for America' is harmful to both students and teachers. He then uncovers the ineffectiveness of recycling and calls out the billionaires who create charities for tax breaks.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Immigration
programme.desc: Adam illustrates the big problem a border wall wouldn't address, explores the history of racist deportations, and explains how immigration courts are a huge mess.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Housing
programme.desc: While looking for a new home, Adam exposes the harsh realities of real estate. Plus, he explains why helping the homeless is a lot simpler than you think.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Drugs
programme.desc: Adam weeds through the myths of marijuana, exposes the blunt truth about the War on Drugs, and explains how prescription pills are the true gateway drug.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything: Adam Ruins Prison
programme.desc: Adam and Eve shed light on jail rehabilitation, solitary confinement and the fact that corporations make money off of prison inmates.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Riverside Stakeout

programme.title: North Woods Law: Good News, Bad News
programme.desc: Officers St. Pierre, Benvenuti and K9 Cora investigate a couple accused of road hunting. An actress tries to perform her way out of a ticket with Officer Hannett. Officer Holmes gets an injured goose call and Officer Frye handles a neighbor dispute.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Uncuffed: Mount Rescue
programme.desc: Officer Holmes and Trainee Frye race to save a man suffering from a severe head injury after an ATV accident. Two officers attempt a treacherous rescue on Mount Washington, while a collapsed student needs help on Mount Willard.

programme.title: Northwest Law: Poacher Pursuit
programme.desc: The fish and wildlife officers of Washington State are on the hunt for a set of poachers, one is a repeat offender targeting crabs, while another is caught attempting to reel in an endangered species.

programme.title: Northwest Law: The Old Men and The Lake

programme.title: North Woods Law: Stranded and Abandoned
programme.desc: Officers Lucas and Cyrs attempt a treacherous rescue on Mt. Washington, while Lt. Boudreau tracks down the owner of an abandoned vehicle. Officer Hannett and Trainee Crouse investigate a man shooting squirrels. Officer Fluette responds to a bear call.

programme.title: The Crocodile Hunter: Reptiles of the Deep: Saurians
programme.desc: The Irwins travel to the waters off Jamaica, Vanuatu and Queensland, Australia, to swim mere inches from the massive jaws of the reptiles that inhabit these deep ocean waters. Steve and Terri search the Galapagos Islands for marine iguanas.

programme.title: Too Cute!: Puppies Making Mischief

programme.title: My Cat From Hell: Graveyard of Peed on Things
programme.desc: A one-eyed cerebral hypoplasia cat, won't stop tormenting and attacking fellow cat, Red.

programme.title: The Vet Life: Hog House Call

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet: A Horse of a Different Color

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: My Way
programme.desc: Commander Perry notifies the Intelligence Unit that Pulpo has been released to help track down a dangerous cartel leader. Meanwhile, Ruzek gets some difficult news as a blast from Lindsay's past comes back.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: The Docks
programme.desc: As Antonio's life is now in question, Voight and Platt open up about their pasts. Jin is caught while tinkering with Voight's computer and uses the only scapegoat possible to keep him in the clear.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: A Beautiful Friendship
programme.desc: Atwater is settling into his new gig on intelligence as Antonio is relegated to desk duty while recovering. Lindsay finally realizes she has no way out and confides in Voight for help.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Call It Macaroni
programme.desc: Jin's murder investigation moves forward and Voight is under close watch by Internal Affairs, but is still in complete command of his unit.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Get My Cigarettes
programme.desc: Intelligence begins to look into a string of murders where the victims have a dollar bill pinned to their chests. Thanks to some digging by Ruzek, Voight learns this friend was involved in some shady business dealings.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: The Weigh Station
programme.desc: The bounty on Halstead's head becomes a serious reality as an innocent bartender takes a bullet meant for him. Olinsky tries to reason with Bembenek to call off the hit, while Voight and his team search for the hired assassin.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: Chicken, Dynamite, Chainsaw
programme.desc: Two teenage girls have gone missing, and the team knows the clock is ticking to find them. Halstead, Antonio and Lindsay investigate a tenement that gives them a much needed lead. Voight brings the father of one of the girls in to get some honest answers.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.: An Honest Woman
programme.desc: While trying to help out Olive, Voight is attacked and kidnapped. When he does not show up for work, the team gets suspicious and begins investigating. Ruzek and Atwater prove they do belong on the team when they find a lead.

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles: Home Is Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: After a maintenance man saved the life of a lieutenant commander, the team uncovers his dark past which puts a local teenager in danger. Also, Sam must decide which colleague to take to the Los Angeles football game.

programme.title: Dateline: A Crack in Everything
programme.desc: A woman's death is ruled a suicide until a new investigation forces police to question if it is a case of murder.

programme.title: Snapped: Angelina Rodrigues
programme.desc: A sudden illness ends a short marriage, the widow could be hiding a poisonous secret.

programme.title: Snapped: Pamela Ballin
programme.desc: When a man is beaten to death in his own home, police search for intruders but find a suspect much closer to home.

programme.title: Dateline: Secrets Uncovered: Suspicion
programme.desc: The neighbors you’re about to meet enjoyed mixing business with pleasure then a murder made them face the reality that a killer was in their midst.

programme.title: Dateline: Secrets Uncovered: Buried Secrets
programme.desc: Judy Carlson lived in a painful limbo. Her son disappeared without a trace. Years went by and she may never have known his fate if not for a rookie detective’s dogged work but to solve this cold case, the investigator also need the help of a killer.

programme.title: License to Kill: Deadly God Complex
programme.desc: A Dallas spine surgeon's God complex puts patients at risk. It's up to fellow medical staff to blow the whistle before more innocent people are victimized.

programme.title: License to Kill: Dying for a Cure
programme.desc: After receiving a terminal cancer diagnosis, families turn to a doctor who offers hope and an unorthodox cure. But, they soon learn that the trusted physician may have actually had a hand in their loved one's demise.

programme.title: License to Kill: Lethal Injections
programme.desc: An alarming spike in ICU patient deaths prompts a head nurse to investigate the cause. After examining charts recorded by the hospital staff, her findings send shockwaves through the small Indiana town.

programme.title: License to Kill: Deadly Dialysis
programme.desc: Dialysis patients' weekly treatments go from routine to fatal without explanation. Police shut down the Lufkin, TX, facility to investigate what, or who, may have turned lethal.

programme.title: License to Kill: Killing Everything
programme.desc: After an orderly is arrested for killing a patient at a Cincinnati hospital, a local reporter gets a tip that leads him to uncover new information about a string of horrifying deaths.

programme.title: License to Kill: Homicidal Homecare
programme.desc: A beloved grandfather vanishes out of the blue. Strange activity in his financial records leads detectives on a chase from California to Mexico, where they reach a devastating conclusion.

programme.title: License to Kill: Murder by Syringe
programme.desc: A Glendale, CA, hospital worker confesses to suffocating dozens of his patients. Investigators fear he'll walk free because doctors believe the victims died of natural causes.

programme.title: Snapped: Raynella Leath
programme.desc: A widow and her stepdaughter accuse each other of murder, but only one goes on trial for her life.

programme.title: Movie: The Mummy
programme.desc: While investigating a newly discovered Egyptian tomb, a team of explorers accidentally releases the evil mummified princess that was imprisoned within. Having only grown more malevolent and more evil over time, the mummy sets out to reshape the world.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Fatzcarraldo
programme.desc: When all the fast food restaurants in Springfield become healthy, Homer turns to the last bastion of greasy food for comfort and digs up some childhood memories along the way.

programme.title: The Simpsons: The Cad and the Hat
programme.desc: When Bart betrays Lisa, he has to deal with his guilt literally. Meanwhile, Springfield is in awe when Homer is revealed to be a chess savant.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Kamp Krustier
programme.desc: When Bart and Lisa return home abruptly after a traumatic incident at Kamp Krustier, they put an end to Homer and Marge's romantic encounters. Without sex to distract him, Homer becomes a more productive worker and caring husband.

programme.title: The Simpsons: 22 for 30
programme.desc: In this parody of a cable sports show, Bart goes from delinquent with detention to the star basketball player at Springfield Elementary. Lisa covers Bart's success for the school paper, and Homer becomes the team's coach.

programme.title: The Simpsons: A Father's Watch
programme.desc: Worried that Bart is destined for failure, Marge turns to a series of parenting 'experts' for advice. Homer decides to cash in on one expert’s advice by opening up a trophy store in Springfield.

programme.title: The Simpsons: Caper Chase
programme.desc: Disappointed in Yale, Mr. Burns decides to start his own for-profit university and Homer is hired as a professor.

programme.title: Family Guy: The Woof of Wall Street
programme.desc: Brian convinces Stewie to teach him how to invest in the stock market after learning Stewie has a knack for making money from it. Meanwhile, Peter, Quagmire, Cleveland and Joe look after the Drunken Clam while Jerome is away.

programme.title: Family Guy: No Giggity, No Doubt

programme.title: Animals Gone Wild: Cheating Death
programme.desc: An elephant has a run-in with a carload of tourists. A lion shows his natural instincts can’t be tamed. A tiny Chihuahua takes on armed robbers. These unforgettable brushes with death remind us that everyday survival can be precarious.

programme.title: Animals Gone Wild: The Bold and the Brutal
programme.desc: From shocking head-to-head battles and terrifying brushes with death, to sudden ambush assaults and impossible breakout escapes, these are battles like you’ve never seen before.

programme.title: Animals Gone Wild: Epic Fails
programme.desc: From fearsome surprise attacks and bone-crushing collisions, to animals pushed too far and predators in too deep. These are the animal world’s most treacherous mistakes on Animals Gone Wild 2: Epic Fails.

programme.title: Dangerous Encounters: Deadliest Sharks
programme.desc: Dr. Brady Barr studies dangerous creatures in their natural environments.

programme.title: Dangerous Encounters: Robo-Snake
programme.desc: Brady investigates technology such as bomb disposal robots, used by others with dangerous jobs, to see if he can use them in his work to keep him safe when catching deadly snakes.

programme.title: Dangerous Encounters: Sawfish Giant
programme.desc: Dr. Brady Barr studies dangerous creatures in their natural environments.

programme.title: Dangerous Encounters: Bayou Beasts
programme.desc: Brady is on a mission to find the alligator snapping turtle, one of the largest turtles in the world. The alligator snapping turtle is true historic beast armed with vice like jaws capable of dismembering their prey.

programme.title: Dangerous Encounters: Home Invasion
programme.desc: Dr. Brady Barr investigates the animals that raid our suburbs, dumpsters and homes and tests a few theories that might keep them from becoming backyard bandits.

programme.title: Predator in Paradise: In the Shallows
programme.desc: Predators fight for survival in tropical paradise.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Missing: Atlantis: The Lost Evidence
programme.desc: Atlantis is one of the most famous disappearances of all time. After thousands of years, state-of-the-art technology might finally be able to crack this ancient mystery. And after nine hikers die in a gruesome cold case, new science provides a hot lead.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Missing: Hunt for Flight MH370
programme.desc: Since 2014, the fate of Malaysia Airlines flight MH370 has remained a mystery. It's still unclear if it crashed, and if it did, the wreckage has yet to be found.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Missing: Curse of the Bermuda Triangle
programme.desc: After five planes vanish in the Bermuda Triangle, new science suggests a strange geological force is responsible. Today, satellites are revealing lost tribes living in the Amazon jungle, but some civilizations remain missing, stumping experts.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Missing: Lost Colony of Roanoke
programme.desc: The Roanoke colony vanished without a trace, and centuries later, it remains one of history's greatest unsolved mysteries. But new evidence might at last reveal its fate. After a man is swallowed by the earth, experts wonder if a sinkhole is to blame.

programme.title: Unearthed: Egypt's City of the Gods
programme.desc: New discoveries reveal the dark secrets of ancient Egypt's holiest and most mysterious cities, where the pharaohs practiced strange rituals that might have helped their rise to power.

programme.title: Unearthed: Mysteries of the Monuments
programme.desc: New findings reveal the secrets behind some of the world's most iconic monuments.

programme.title: Forbidden History: Lost Treasures of the Holy Land
programme.desc: The Holy Land is home to some of the most mysterious relics, and new discoveries reveal the secrets of these strange and sacred artifacts.

programme.title: Unexplained and Unexplored: Ghost Ship of the Damned
Unearthed: Vesuvius's Secret Victim
programme.desc: Scientists are using cutting edge technology to unearth a second city that, like the well-known city of Pompeii, was destroyed when Vesuvius erupted in 79 AD. This new town is revealed to be one of the most well-preserved Roman towns on earth.

programme.title: Forbidden History: Dead Sea Scrolls Conspiracy
programme.desc: Experts investigated the Dead Sea Scrolls, which have been kept top-secret for decades by the Vatican. These could reveal the location of tunnel holding billions of dollars of treasure.

programme.title: Unexplained and Unexplored: Ghost Ship of the Damned
Unearthed: Pompeii's Doomsday Mummies
programme.desc: New discoveries reveal dark secrets of the event that destroyed Pompeii.

programme.title: Deadly Engineering: Doom on the Titanic
programme.desc: New discoveries are challenging everything we know about the sinking of the Titanic, revealing that the disaster wasn't caused by a freak accident and that it may have been doomed from the start.

programme.title: Deadly Engineering: Nightmare in Hell Valley
programme.desc: After torrential rainfall triggers a catastrophic landslide, a small village in the valley below is devastated, and now, new discoveries reveal the truth behind the engineering that led to this fatal disaster.

programme.title: Deadly Engineering: The Chernobyl Conspiracy
programme.desc: The Chernobyl nuclear disaster was a catastrophe that changed the course of human history, and now, new discoveries reveal the real reasons behind the explosion that spawned a deadly radioactive ghost town and brought down an empire.

programme.title: Deadly Engineering: NASA's Challenger Disaster
programme.desc: NASA's Space Shuttle Challenger was a deadly disaster that changed the course of space exploration forever, and now, new discoveries reveal the truth behind the structural failure that tore the rocket apart.

programme.title: Deadly Engineering: Cruise Ship Catastrophe
programme.desc: When a cruise ship meets disaster, the ship begins to sink and the captain flees, leaving 300 passengers stranded; now, new discoveries reveal the fatal truth behind the devastating wreck that claimed 32 lives.

programme.title: How It's Made: Oil Lamps, Chocolate Mints, Underfloor Heating, Pillows
programme.desc: See how oil lamps, chocolate mints, under floor heating and pillows are made.

programme.title: How It's Made: Ski Bindings, Immersion Parts Washers, Mine Ventilation, Pencil Sharpeners
programme.desc: This episode includes ski bindings, immersion parts washers, mine ventilation and pencil sharpeners.

programme.title: How It's Made: Carbon Fiber Bicycles, Blood Products, Forged Chandeliers And Ballpoint Pen
programme.desc: Find out how carbon fiber bicycles, blood products, forged chandeliers and ballpoint pens are made.

programme.title: How It's Made: Drill Bits, Photo Booths, Stamps
programme.desc: Learn how drill bits, photo booths and stamps are made.

programme.title: How It's Made: Ice Cream Cones, Tent Trailers, Shoe Polish and Pliers

programme.title: How It's Made: Life Casting; Downdraft Cooktops; Compression Hosiery; Electric Motorcycles
programme.desc: Body casting; downdraft stoves; compression garments; electric motorcycles.

programme.title: Mega Machines: Sea Giants: Titans of Power

programme.title: Mega Machines: Sea Giants: Masters of the Deep

programme.desc: A sleepy New England beach town is the backdrop to murder, when a young mother-to-be is found tied up and strangled. It takes years but a chance encounter leads investigators down a long winding path of misleads and greed before they find the real killer.

programme.title: Truth About Murder With Sunny Hostin: Always Be Mine
programme.desc: A young mother is shot execution-style in her kitchen while her daughter cries in the next room. It will take a full team effort from the investigators and prosecution to wade through the intricate web of lies the killer weaves to reveal the truth.

programme.title: Relatively Evil: Living Lies
programme.desc: A picture-perfect marriage ends with a horrific murder.While the killer puts the blame on cold medicine, investigators suspect a different diagnosis.

programme.title: Truth About Murder With Sunny Hostin: Knock Twice for Murder
programme.desc: A sleepy New England beach town is the backdrop to murder, when a young mother-to-be is found tied up and strangled. It takes years but a chance encounter leads investigators down a long winding path of misleads and greed before they find the real killer.

programme.title: Truth About Murder With Sunny Hostin: Always Be Mine
programme.desc: A young mother is shot execution-style in her kitchen while her daughter cries in the next room. It will take a full team effort from the investigators and prosecution to wade through the intricate web of lies the killer weaves to reveal the truth.

programme.title: Home Sweet Homicide: Footsteps in the Snow
programme.desc: A young mother is found brutally murdered in her own home in Chestnut Ridge, New York. Clues outside the house have footprints leading in, but none leading out.

programme.title: Blood Relatives: Sex, Lies and Videotape
programme.desc: A frantic 9-1-1 caller informs police he's found a family member dead. At first, it looks like a simple case of suicide. But a deeper dig reveals a secret world of sex tapes, broken promises and unbelievable murder motives.

programme.title: Blood Relatives: The Four Horsemen
programme.desc: Bigfork, Montana is known for producing hearty men. Some are so rugged they seem programmed for murder. This tiny town will soon face not one--but two--murders, that appear random but are actually connected in a shocking way.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: Children of Thunder
programme.desc: In the summer of 2000, panic overwhelms the Bay Area when bags full of body parts start appearing all over the region. Investigators follow the trail of grisly clues and it leads to the discovery of a secret cult carrying out a sinister scheme.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: Monster in the Desert
programme.desc: In 1992, a 19-year-old woman is brutally assaulted outside of Desert Hot Springs, California. Miraculously she escapes, but lives in constant fear that he will find her again one day. What the victim doesn't realize is she survived a serial killer.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates: Where Is Baby DeOrr?
programme.desc: A young couple enjoys an impromptu camping trip in Leadore, Idaho, until their 2-year-old son suddenly vanishes. Investigators and private detectives work around the clock to find the missing boy, only to point the finger back at the parents.

programme.title: 20/20 on ID: Bad Blood
programme.desc: There's no body. No murder weapon. But police believe a beloved wife and mother is dead and that the killer is her husband, who insists he's innocent. Prosecutors take him to court four times, trying to unravel the truth about a haunting disappearance.

programme.title: 20/20 on ID: BFFs From Hell
programme.desc: Skylar Neece's parents thought Shelia and Rachel were their 16 year-old daughter's best friends. One night Skylar climbed out her bedroom window and never returned. They began to wonder if the two BFFs were her mortal enemies.

programme.title: 20/20 on ID: Left to Die
programme.desc: Toddler Cooper Harris dies after being left in a hot car. His father says it was an accident. Authorities say it was murder. What secrets was the father keeping? The grieving mother who stunned a courtroom speaks out about what happened to her baby boy.

programme.title: Murder Loves Company: Killer Coverup
programme.desc: A beloved friend and co-worker goes missing on a desolate mountain, and detectives must sort through a list of tightknit colleagues with potential motives for murder. One of them has crucial information that will lead to a killer amongst them.

programme.title: Murder Loves Company: Friends to the End
programme.desc: A close-knit group of co-workers are devastated when one of their own is found dead near a soccer field at the edge of their quiet Indiana town.

programme.title: Murder Loves Company: Dorm Living Nightmare
programme.desc: A girls' movie night gone bad leads to a not so happy ending for this group of college roommates. Police investigate a murder that leads them halfway across the state in search of the killer.

programme.title: Murder Loves Company: Devil in the Dance Hall
programme.desc: A murder within the small square dancing community of Texarkana, Arkansas leads investigators through a gauntlet of suspects with alibies and potential motives- including love triangles, jealousy and lots of money.

programme.title: Chasing Conspiracies: Cold War in Space
programme.desc: July 21st, 1969 saw one of the iconic events of the 20th century when the Apollo 10 crew set foot on the moon. However, some claim the moon landings were faked. A hacker claims to have found evidence of a U.S. space fleet fueled by alien technology.

programme.title: Codes and Conspiracies: Gold
programme.desc: A look at the history of gold, where most of it resides today and who owns it.

programme.title: CIA Declassified: Fortress of War
programme.desc: The Story of how two CIA operatives, in search of Osama bin Laden, stop a Taliban uprising in an Afghan fortress. They are outnumbered leaving one operative to pay the ultimate price.

programme.title: CIA Declassified: The Beirut Bomb
programme.desc: The true story of how CIA Director William Casey allegedly sanctioned an off-the-books assassination attempt on Sheikh Mohammed Fadlallah in Beirut in 1985. Casey was convinced Fadlallah was the head of Hezbollah and behind a series of terrorist attacks.

programme.title: Codes and Conspiracies: Deadly Cults
programme.desc: Discover some of America's most infamous cults. Whether its religious fire-and -brimstone, Armageddon, or visitors from outer space, the US has no shortage of like-minded people coming together to get swept up in the fervour.

programme.title: Manson: The Prison Tapes
programme.desc: Relive the horrific Tate and LaBianca murders, and the media circus surrounding the trials of cult leader Charles Manson and his 'family' of misfit killers.

programme.title: Waco Standoff: As We Watched
programme.desc: On April 19, 1993, FBI agents attacked a religious compound in Waco, Texas, occupied by a small obscure religious cult. 76 people, including 26 children, died. The repercussions of Waco are still being felt.

programme.title: Unabomber: 20 Years of Terror
programme.desc: Unabomber Ted Kaczynski terrorized the country with mail bombs but when his brother David realized who was the Unabomber, he made a hard decision to turn Ted over to authorities. David shares his dramatic story with us and retraces the Unabomber's steps.

programme.title: Manson: The Prison Tapes

programme.title: Ancient Assassins: The Pope's Bodyguards
programme.desc: From stone-age Hawaii to 16th-century Japan, from Ancient Greece to the Byzantine Empire, there have always been elite Special Forces.

programme.title: Ancient Assassins: Beautiful Samurai
programme.desc: In 12th Century Japan, a female samurai humiliates all the others. As lethal as she was lovely, Tomoe Gozen defeated the finest swordsmen, battled tyranny and was innovative on the battlefield.

programme.title: How the World Ends: Nuclear Apocalypse
programme.desc: The Cold War might be long gone, but the nuclear threat has re-emerged. Meet nuclear preparedness enthusiasts as they dig their bunkers and stockpile supplies, determined to survive a threat that could become a global apocalypse.

programme.title: WWII: Masters of War: Winston Churchill
programme.desc: May 28, 1940, while the Wehrmacht crushes the allied armies in France, British officials prepare to face Hitler.

programme.title: WWII: Masters of War: Franklin D. Roosevelt
programme.desc: Japanese bombers attack Pearl Harbor and sink a large part of the US Pacific Fleet. President Franklin D. Roosevelt decides it's time for the United States to strike back.

programme.title: WWII: Masters of War: Charles de Gaulle
programme.desc: June 16, 1940. Paris has fallen to the Nazis and the German conquest of France is almost complete. Prime Minister Henri Petain plans to sign an armistice with Nazi Germany, but French General Charles de Gaulle secedes and rebels against the policy.

programme.title: WWII: Masters of War: Adolf Hitler
programme.desc: On June 22, 1941, Adolf Hitler launches the largest military offensive in history against the Soviet Union: Operation Barbarossa. Blinded by his fire power, Hitler signs his death sentence.

programme.title: Codes and Conspiracies: The Vatican
programme.desc: The Vatican is a secretive, watchful institution that protects its reputation and the members of its ranks with fierce, often reckless abandon. The secrets and scandals throughout its history are some of the darkest.

programme.title: Chasing Conspiracies: The Kennedy Killings
programme.desc: Conspiracy theories surrounding the assassination of Robert Kennedy, the murder of TV presenter Jill Dando and the death of government weapons inspector Dr. David Kelly are examined.

programme.title: Chasing Conspiracies: The Alien Files
programme.desc: The final edition of the series examines a range of conspiracy theories relating to extra-terrestrials. There are those who believe that we have already made contact with aliens and that world governments are concealing this.

programme.title: Renovation Realities: The Tews Job
programme.desc: Matt and Tina are ready to make their outdated, barely functional master bathroom beautiful again. However, their execution of this project turns out to be much harder than either of them expected.

programme.title: Renovation Realities: The Threadgould Job
programme.desc: Best friends tear down an outdated kitchen to make room for a new one.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Big Sky Ranch: The Build Begins
programme.desc: After years spent traveling and working around the globe, Jon and Etta Smith are settling down with their 18-month-old daughter, Syndi. In one of the most remote corners of northwestern Montana, they are building a home and bison ranch off the grid.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Big Sky Ranch: The Race Against Winter
programme.desc: Jon and Etta Smith's build is under way, and they're working to balance ranch and build duties. In Montana's northwest, they have little time to waste, winter is severe, and it's coming fast.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Big Sky Ranch: The Bison Comes Home
programme.desc: Jon and Etta are in a race against time on their Montana house build, but it's not just the house that has them working around the clock: they're also building a bison ranch from the ground up.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Big Sky Ranch: Montana Time
programme.desc: Jon and Etta Smith have built their off-the-grid house from the foundation up to the roof, but this late into the winter, they're forced to deal with bitter cold and high winds every day.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Big Sky Ranch: Teamwork Makes the Dream Work
programme.desc: In a mad dash to finish the exterior of their house before another big Montana storm hits, Jon and Etta's interior work has been put on hold. With cold temperatures and little daylight to work with, every baby step they make is a victory in its own right.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Big Sky Ranch: Dreams Become Reality
programme.desc: After construction delays pushed construction into the dead of winter, Jon and Etta Smith are nearing the end of the build. The finishing touches, including the custom stone fireplace, solar panels, and furniture are put into place.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Bottle Island
programme.desc: John Beal is ready to leave the city behind and embrace a life off the grid in Isla Mujeres, Mexico. He hires Richart Sowa and his son Eden to build an eco-friendly private floating island made from recycled plastic bottles.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Streamside Cottage
programme.desc: Outlaw architect Michael Reynolds doesn't just live off the grid, he builds off the grid.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Mountain Timber Frame
programme.desc: A man builds an off-grid timber frame cabin on his family's 100-acre property in upstate New York.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Hawaiian Hideaway
programme.desc: Innovative designer and builder Graham Hill sets out to transform a steep Maui jungle plot into a 600-square-foot, hi-tech dream home with multiple moving walls and other space-maximizing features.

programme.title: Disaster Decks: The Deck Pam Hates
programme.desc: Don and Pam are close to retiring and want to be able to enjoy the next 20 years together in their beautiful backyard, but their 25-year-old deck is unsafe and needs a complete takedown and rebuild.

programme.title: Paid Program
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Heritage Hunters: A Living Room Waterfall
programme.desc: In a show first, Bug and the team deconstruct a historic boat with a lot of salvage potential.

programme.title: Heritage Hunters: Egg Feeder Barn Doors
programme.desc: Bug and the gang are tasked with one of the most dangerous salvage projects to date: bringing down a structure without damaging the building it's attached to.

programme.title: Heritage Hunters: Hard Wood and Twisted Metal
programme.desc: Bug and Steve find a rare redwood water tank that has tons of potential, if they can salvage it properly. A new client stops by the yard looking to design an intricate hardwood feature for their home.

programme.title: Heritage Hunters: Hometown Touch
programme.desc: Bug and the team are tasked with reclaiming materials from a historic vineyard that can turn a huge profit if they make it out unscathed.

programme.title: Heritage Hunters: Mare Island Naval Yard
programme.desc: Bug and his team help transform a client’s kitchen with salvaged Douglas fir from the historic Mare Island Naval Yard.

programme.title: Heritage Hunters: Pennsylvania Charm Meets California Pool House
programme.desc: The team at Heritage Salvage works hard to salvage as much as they can from their biggest brooder barn to date. Back at the shipyard, Bug comes up with a plan to use a hand-hewn beam from a Pennsylvania Amish barn.

programme.title: Heritage Hunters: Same Footprint, Bigger Kitchen
programme.desc: Bug and the team discover a treasure trove filled with salvageable old growth redwood, but the danger might outweigh the reward.

programme.title: Heritage Hunters: The Golden Oak Menace
programme.desc: Bug and his team travel to West Petaluma, CA, to take down a 95-year-old egg house that's dangerously close to a high-voltage power line.

programme.title: Heritage Hunters: Wine Stave Porch Railing
programme.desc: Bug and his team attempt a large, stubborn tear down that could be their biggest score ever, if the materials are even salvageable. Then, the gang takes on a front porch renovation that forces them to get creative with 18th-century Victorian materials.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Northeast Farmhouse
programme.desc: Mike, Robert, and the Black Dog Salvage crew save parts of an early 1800s farmhouse and barn in Edison, NJ. Key items include corner plinths, thick floorboards, and an elaborate front entryway. Mike also builds a daybed swing with spindles.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: P.A. Denny Riverboat
programme.desc: The Dawgs save a calliope from a riverboat in Parkersburg, WV. They also split into teams to pick through the owner's large warehouse.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: American Viscose Plant
programme.desc: The Dawgs save a kiln from the American Viscose Plant in Roanoke, VA. Mike and Tay work together to build custom tables for a local optometrist office. Will Farmer also returns to appraise a map from 1853.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Taylor Family Farm
programme.desc: Picking through old farm land and former Native American settlement, the crew find several treasures to load up and take back to Roanoke. Mike and Tay build a freight cart couch in the shop and Robert gets excited about a corn sheller.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Twin Capes Ferry
programme.desc: The crew heads to Norfolk, VA, to test their sea legs and salvage an obsolete ferry including lights, skylights and a mural. Back at the shop, Grayson helps Mike and Tay with a commissioned build for a fellow maker.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Vaughan Furniture Company
programme.desc: Mike and Ted struggle to salvage a heavy furniture press while Robert is slowed down by explosion-proof lights. Mike and Grayson build a chair with pieces from an old suitcase and Robert discovers a vintage TV and radio.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: 19th Century Corncrib
programme.desc: The Salvage Dawgs go to Hamburg, PA, to carefully deconstruct a corncrib being sure to take plenty of notes and pictures so they can reassemble it accurately.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Beechcraft Airplane Salvage
programme.desc: The Salvage Dawgs try something a little different by bidding on a 1947 Beechcraft airplane. Mike goes a little over budget to win the auction and the crew has to hire professionals to figure out how to disassemble it properly.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Burlington Wall Unit
programme.desc: The teams visits Burlington, NC, to save an old apothecary unit from a 1930s pharmacy. Then, Mike and Robert search for some new patterns for the shop in West Virginia, while Grayson builds a console.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Backyard Blowouts
programme.desc: Roger's in Southern California for two unbelievable backyard blowouts. Roger meets Chef Ben Ford in Tarzana.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Cajun Cookouts
programme.desc: Roger heads to the Texas and Louisiana to feast on classic Cajun cooking.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Holy Smokes!
programme.desc: Roger attends the 100 year anniversary of the St. Mary Magdalene Church Picnic in Owensboro, Kentucky. Several thousand pounds of meat will cook over 100 feet of fire and smoke.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Pit Masters
programme.desc: In Lexington, Texas folks line up early on Saturday mornings for Snow's BBQ.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Monster Meat
programme.desc: Roger Mooking heads to Hoodoo Brown Barbeque in Ridgefield, CT, where owner Cody Sperry serves up monster-sized meaty masterpieces. Cody serves up "outlaw barbeque," a mashup of styles and traditions with smoke pork butts, pork ribs, pork belly and beef.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: BBQ and Boils in the Bayou
programme.desc: Roger Mooking visits the Louisiana bayou for barbecue, brunch and a crawfish boil. In Prairieville, L.A., he helps a husband-and-wife team of caterers prepare baby back ribs, brisket and 200 pounds of live crawfish.

programme.title: Man's Greatest Food: Barbecue
programme.desc: Roger Mooking cannot resist the sweet, succulent smoky goodness of barbecue as he canvasses the country on the lookout for the fifteen best barbecue dishes wherever he can find them.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Extra Meat Please
programme.desc: Michael Symon starts in Queens, New York, with a burger loaded to the max with beef, ham, bacon and sausage.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Burnt Ends For Breakfast
programme.desc: Michael Symon heads to Plainview, N.Y., where a chocolate bacon burger is loaded from top to bottom with sweet and savory indulgence. A restaurant in Columbus, Ohio, has a soy-marinated pineapple hanger steak draped over spicy kimchi fried rice.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Triple Meat Trifecta
programme.desc: In Newport, R.I., Michael Symon dives into a triple meat-stacked sandwich with smoked pork belly, bologna and chicken liver pate. A decadent, ultra-crispy and deep-fried soft-shell crab patty in San Francisco will change the way you look at burgers.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Dangerously Good Eats
programme.desc: Chef Michael Symon sinks his teeth into a burger in Memphis, Tenn., that's so dangerously good, innocent bystanders had better beware! Then pizza gets a brunch makeover in Atlanta with creamy scrambled eggs and crispy pancetta.

programme.title: Man v. Food: San Antonio, Texas
programme.desc: Casey Webb's tasty travels take him to San Antonio, Texas. His first stop is San Antonio's oldest restaurant- a German deli- for the Wienerschnitzel.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Phoenix, AZ
programme.desc: Casey heads to Arizona to unearth the Phoenix's most appetizing eats and take on eight wings slathered in a scorching ghost chili and habanero sauce plus two whole grilled habanero peppers.

programme.title: Budget Battle: Pop Rock ’n Roll
programme.desc: Four budget conscious chefs compete to transform affordable ingredients into culinary masterpieces. Tackling cupboard staples like canned beef hash, beef jerky, and canned soup for breakfast.

programme.title: Late Nite Eats: Montreal
programme.desc: Jordan Andino explores the Montreal food scene and it doesn't disappoint. He heads to Notre Dame de Grace to try the late night discount menu at Monkland Tavern. Jordan falls in love with the braised calamari caponata.

programme.title: Late Nite Eats: Boston
programme.desc: Jordan Andino is in Beantown and starting the night in Southie for some tempting Korean Tacos and a surprise sweet at Moonshine 152. Next, he's off to Lord Hobo in Cambridge where he tries their beer-infused Chicago-style corn dogs.

programme.title: Late Nite Eats: Toronto
programme.desc: Jordan Andino is exploring the culinary delights of Toronto, Canada's most diverse city.

programme.title: Late Nite Eats: Oakland
programme.desc: Chef Jordan Andino checks out the Bay Area's biggest city, Oakland, California, and makes his first stop at Seawolf Public House for classic comfort foods with international flavors.

programme.title: Late Nite Eats: North Jersey
programme.desc: Jordan Andino kicks off his night in northern New Jersey with coconut crab buns at Dullboy in Jersey City. For his next stop, he's headed just a few blocks away to Talde for Filipino-style short ribs.

programme.title: Late Nite Eats: Portland
programme.desc: Jordan Andino hits the ground running in Portland, Maine, a tiny New England town with a lot to offer late at night. He starts off at Nosh, a late night hangout known for supersized sandwiches and over-the-top snack food.

programme.title: Cake Hunters: Basketball or Bach?
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Reid is about to celebrate his Bar Mitzvah. The party will include one hundred of Reid's closest friends and relatives. Mom and Reid's little sister Sloan want a cake that highlights Reid's talent for music.

programme.title: Cake Hunters: Elephant in the Room
programme.desc: Brothers Sumit and Swithin are both recently engaged, so their mother Rani is throwing a dual traditional Indian engagement party with over 300 guests. Mom has her heart set on a traditional Indian show-stopper cake, but the brothers, not so much.

programme.title: Cake Hunters: Double Trouble
programme.desc: Katie and Jason are not only expecting identical twins, but also dozens of friends and family to celebrate with them at their baby gender reveal party.

programme.title: Cake Hunters: Carnival Dreams
programme.desc: Mike and Miranda have always shared a love of fun and are planning a carnival-themed wedding, an homage to their first date, but the cake design is still up in the air.

programme.title: Unwrapped 2.0: Southern Treats
programme.desc: In the land of Dixie, see how a few of the favorite sweet Southern treats are made. Watch GooGoo Clusters take a bath in chocolate; Red Bird Peppermint Puffs earn their stripes, and find out the sweet secret behind Carolina Foods' Honeybun.

programme.title: Unwrapped 2.0: Barbecue Sides
programme.desc: Alfonso Ribeiro puts together the perfect backyard barbecue, including Mrs. Freshley's melt-in-your-mouth cupcakes, Mrs. Fisher's perfectly-salted rippled chips, and Mayfield Dairy's creamy banana split ice cream.

programme.title: Unwrapped 2.0: Nostalgic Eats
programme.desc: Host Alfonso Ribeiro takes your taste buds back in time to see how the classic Necco Wafers are made.

programme.title: Unwrapped 2.0: Play With Your Food
programme.desc: Alfonso says it's okay to play with your food, especially these tasty treats! From super-sized Fatboy ice cream sandwiches to super-seasoned Wise Onion rings to playful Melville candy glitter mustache lollipops.

programme.title: Charmed: Mr. and Mrs. Witch

programme.desc: When Henry's parolee, Nick, crashes his car into a bank after being denied a loan, Paige and Billie must come up with a non-magical solution. Elsewhere, Piper and Phoebe have their hands full when Wyatt uses his magic to make his toys come to life.

programme.title: Movie: A Holiday Engagement

programme.desc: A reporter pretends to be the girlfriend of the son of a famous politician to help him save face over Christmas. She becomes torn between exposing newsworthy scoops about the famous family and trusting that she's falling in love for real.

programme.title: Movie: Mingle All the Way
programme.desc: A young entrepreneur invents a new dating app designed to help singles find dates to events. She personally tests it out in the weeks before Christmas and is connected with a like-minded individual whom she's already dated, several times.

programme.title: Movie: Christmas Cookies

programme.title: Movie: Miss Christmas

programme.title: Movie: A Bride for Christmas

programme.title: South Park: Butt Out

programme.title: South Park: Crème Fraiche

programme.title: Forensic Files: Material Evidence
programme.desc: Examine intriguing crimes, accidents and outbreaks of disease from around the world.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Kill'igraphy
programme.desc: A man considered unstable by his neighbors is accused of killing his wife, but the medical examiner disagrees.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Death by a Salesman
programme.desc: Even though there were no witnesses, police manage to track down murderer less than 12 hours after the fact. Using fingerprints at the scene and security camera footage, officers arrested the perpetrator even though he was out of the state, on a bus.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Web of Seduction
programme.desc: When the scene of a murder proves to be completely bare of evidence, computer forensics experts manage to dig up files on the victim's computer, and those of his wife and her lover that finger a suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Breaking News
programme.desc: When a young reporter is murdered in her apartment, and the two suspects are cleared of any wrongdoing, police must turn to a databank of well over 8000 felons to catch the killer, leading to the first conviction in the U.S, based on this evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Big Chill
programme.desc: When a woman dies after a long illness, investigators have to determine if her death was due to natural causes, suicide, or murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Mistaken for Dead
programme.desc: A man dies unexpectedly in a doctor's office and the investigation is surprising.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Frozen Evidence
programme.desc: The story of an investigator who captured a shoe impression made in snow.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Treading Not So Lightly
programme.desc: When a four-year-old girl is found unconscious in a parking lot, police concluded it was a hit and run vehicle accident, and left it at that. But the girl's mother was determined to find out exactly what had happened.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Without a Prayer
programme.desc: Madalyn Murray O'Hair and her two top lieutenants disappeared with no shortage of suspects. Her abduction may never have been solved if it weren't for the discovery of a headless, handless body, and the serial number on an artificial hip.

programme.title: Forensic Files: When the Dust Settled
programme.desc: A union official is executed in his home after a strike by union membership, and no one in the neighborhood hears any gunshots.

programme.title: Forensic Files: A Wrong Foot
programme.desc: Illinois police discover a footprint on a birthday card that could lead to the home invader who killed a man and attacked two young girls.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Elephant Tracks
programme.desc: A jeweler notices that a victim wore an elephant pendant that had been pawned in her shop.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Tooth or Consequences
programme.desc: An investigation into the disappearance of a young woman uncovers a tooth.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Fatal Fungus
programme.desc: In Cleveland, more than thirty cases of people suffering from bleeding lungs were reported in the span of a few months. Doctors had never seen anything like it, and searched frantically for the cause and a cure.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Something's Fishy
programme.desc: Examining the cyanide-laced Excedrin killings of 1986, where two people died. When the killer was caught, theirs was the first conviction after federal laws against product tampering were instituted after the Chicago Tylenol murders.

programme.title: The Dead Wives Club: My Wife Is Missing
programme.desc: Arkansas realtor Beverly Carter vanished without a trace. Her husband told police he found her car parked in the driveway of a house she was showing. When her body is discovered in a shallow grave suspicion turns to her husband.

programme.title: Very Scary People: Jim Jones: Unholy Massacre
programme.desc: More than 900 Americans died in an isolated jungle in northern Guyana in November 1978. Jim Jones, has gone down in history as a madman who drove his followers to suicide. But the truth is more complicated and more tragic than that.

programme.title: Strange Evidence: Curse of Poltergeist Hill
programme.desc: When a ghostly encounter in a cemetery is caught on camera, experts use cutting-edge tech and the latest science to investigate if something paranormal is at play.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Cloning the Woolly Mammoth
programme.desc: To help clone the extinct woolly mammoth back to life, Josh Gates visits the Korean biotech lab driving the project and excavates ancient bones in a working Canadian gold mine. He then descends into a vast sinkhole in Siberia to unearth frozen DNA.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Journey to the Ice Age
programme.desc: To find a viable sample of mammoth DNA, Josh Gates retrieves bone samples from the massive Batagay Crater. He then journeys far upriver to a site in the lawless Siberian wilderness where tusk hunters carve out troves of bones from the permafrost.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Lost Gold of Scotland
programme.desc: John joins treasure hunters in the Scottish Highlands chasing a lead on a lost cache of gold that was hidden after the Jacobite Uprising of 1745 and never recovered.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Atlantis of the Andes
programme.desc: Josh dives into a mystery below South America's Lake Titicaca. During the investigation, Josh discovers multiple structures of the ancient Tiwanaku people including ruins hidden in the lake's depths, dubbed 'the Atlantis of the Andes.'

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Rediscovered: Rise and Fall
programme.desc: Josh Gates travels to India in search of a mythical kingdom.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Rediscovered: Beyond The Secret
programme.desc: Josh Gates traverses the US to uncover the mysteries of The Secret.

programme.title: To Be Announced
